feedback.text: It should also protect consumers’ rights when purchasing software, in particular computer games. Software manufacturers often allow the possibility of making the operation of these programmes and games subject to conditions which can be made unachievable unilaterally by the manufacturer. For example, connecting to a server or online service. However, if a game or its content has been paid for, buyers should be allowed to play even if the manufacturer has lost interest in the operation. A purely offline mode or the possibility of producing multi-player content at its own risk, in particular from computer to computer (P2P), could be conceivable. This has already been common in the past.
